title: IP address literals should be valid
messageFormat: This IP address literal is not valid and will throw at runtime.
summary: A constant string passed to IPAddress.Parse is parsed when the line runs, not when it is written, so a typo in the literal stays hidden until that code path executes and then throws a FormatException. Because the value is fixed in source, the mistake can be caught immediately.
Correct the literal so it is a well-formed IPv4 or IPv6 address.
